task.goal: Make a structure with the blueprint below
task.initial_team_instruction: Let's share materials and make a structure with the blueprint

replay_context.execution_state_note: cmd_start is reconstructed from each recorded cmd end timestamp minus duration_ms, then revealed to the critic only when the replay clock reaches that start.

message_timeline.message_text: Need more materials; gathering and finishing it.
message_timeline.local_command_text: !newAction("Finish the blueprint without cheating. First inspect inventory and nearby blocks. Collect enough required blocks: mine nearby stone, stone_bricks, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, polished_granite if present using diamond pickaxe; if polished variants or stone_bricks are unavailable, craft them from stone/andesite/diorite/granite after smelting cobblestone to stone if needed. Also recover any misplaced/extra gold/quartz/glowstone only if needed. Required remaining types include many stone_bricks, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, stone, gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, glowstone. Then place all remaining blueprint fixes exactly at their coordinates from Y64 to Y68, bottom-up, replacing wrong blocks as needed. When done run a blueprint check and fix leftovers.")

!newAction("Collect the missing blueprint materials from nearby blocks without breaking any correctly placed blueprint blocks. Mine nearby gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, glowstone, stone, stone_bricks, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, and polished_granite that are outside the target blueprint coordinates. Then retry placing only the failed/missing blueprint blocks and run checkBlueprint at the end.")

world_changes.attribution_note: Raw replay block changes are not assigned to an executor in this online snapshot.

evidence_boundaries: The snapshot contains no event revealed after window.cutoff_s.
An acting command is execution state, not proof of progress or eventual success.
No active command observed is not sufficient by itself to label an executor idle.
Message recipient and local command executor are separate fields.
Replay block changes remain unattributed unless a separate reliable source identifies the executor.

observations.claim: Bob and Andy are concurrently assigned substantially overlapping collection, blueprint repair, and final-check scopes.
Their inventories support a cleaner specialization: Bob holds gold, quartz, pillar, and glowstone, while Andy's stone-family stock was fully depleted during the window.
Both remain active near the structure, but the team score has not increased since 129.765s; their current commands have overlapped for 41.589s at cutoff.
Partitioning by available material category would reduce duplicate searching and the risk that both executors attempt the same missing blueprint cells.

agent_assessments.summary: Andy is actively gathering and repairing after exhausting substantial stone-family inventory, but progress in the current command is not yet attributable.
Bob is active with a broad finish-all scope and retains the rare decorative materials; unchanged inventory and stale score do not establish either progress or blockage.

manager_advisory.message: Partition the remaining work: Bob should place only gold, quartz, pillar, and glowstone already in his inventory; Andy should gather/place stone-family blocks. Exchange missing coordinates, avoid editing the same cells, and let one agent run the final check.

limitations: Block changes are unattributed, so actual duplicate placements or conflicting edits cannot be confirmed.
Both commands are still running; their eventual results are unavailable at this cutoff.
Inventory depletion does not by itself prove correct placement, and the score is team-level rather than executor-attributed.
